title: MCP Server for Cross-IDE Integration
description: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes agent-bober's orchestration capabilities as tools, enabling Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, and any MCP-compatible IDE to use bober workflows directly from the chat interface. Includes async execution for long-running pipelines with polling.

features.title: MCP Server Core and CLI Entry Point
features.description: Create the MCP server using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk with stdio transport. Register it as a new CLI subcommand: `agent-bober mcp`. The server advertises tool capabilities and handles the JSON-RPC 2.0 lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `npx agent-bober mcp` starts an MCP server that communicates over stdio using JSON-RPC 2.0.
AC2: The server responds to the MCP `initialize` handshake with server name 'agent-bober', version from package.json, and capabilities listing tools.
AC3: The server responds to `tools/list` with a list of registered tool definitions (names, descriptions, input schemas).
AC4: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk is added as a dependency in package.json.
AC5: The project typechecks and builds.

features.title: Core Bober Tools (init, plan, sprint, eval)
features.description: Implement the synchronous MCP tools that map to CLI commands: bober_init, bober_plan, bober_sprint, bober_eval. Each tool wraps the existing command function, captures output, and returns structured results.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: bober_plan tool accepts a 'task' string parameter, calls the planner, and returns the PlanSpec summary with sprint breakdown.
AC2: bober_sprint tool accepts optional 'continue' boolean, executes the next sprint, and returns the sprint result (pass/fail, evaluator feedback).
AC3: bober_eval tool accepts optional 'sprintId' parameter, runs evaluation, and returns the eval result.
AC4: bober_init tool accepts optional 'preset' and 'provider' parameters, initializes the project, and returns confirmation.
AC5: All tools resolve the project root from cwd and load bober.config.json. Missing config produces a clear error instructing the user to run bober_init first.
AC6: The project typechecks, builds, and lints.

features.title: Async Pipeline Tool (bober_run) with Polling
features.description: Implement bober_run as an async tool that starts the full pipeline in the background and returns a run ID immediately. A bober_status tool polls for progress and completion. Uses MCP progress notifications to stream updates.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: bober_run tool accepts a 'task' string, starts the pipeline asynchronously, and immediately returns a JSON response with runId and status 'running'.

AC2: bober_status tool accepts optional 'runId' parameter. When a run is active, it returns current progress (sprints completed/total, current phase, iteration count). When no run is active, it returns the contents of .bober/progress.md.
AC3: When the pipeline completes, bober_status returns the final result (success/failure, completed sprints, failed sprints, duration).
AC4: If bober_run is called while a run is already active, it returns an error 'A pipeline is already running. Use bober_status to check progress.'
AC5: The pipeline run state is tracked in memory (not on disk) since it's per-server-session.
AC6: The project typechecks, builds, and lints.

features.title: Read Tools and Configuration Tools
features.description: Implement read-only MCP tools for inspecting bober state: bober_contracts (list/read sprint contracts), bober_spec (read current plan), bober_principles (read/set project principles), bober_config (read/update bober.config.json).
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: bober_status returns .bober/progress.md content and pipeline run state when called with no arguments.
AC2: bober_contracts tool lists all contracts when called with no arguments. When called with a contractId, returns the full contract JSON.
AC3: bober_spec tool returns the latest PlanSpec JSON from .bober/specs/.
AC4: bober_principles tool reads .bober/principles.md when called with no arguments. When called with a 'content' parameter, writes/updates .bober/principles.md.
AC5: bober_config tool reads bober.config.json when called with no arguments. When called with key-value parameters, updates specific config fields.
AC6: The project typechecks, builds, and lints.

nonFunctional: The MCP server must start in under 2 seconds with no external network calls during initialization.
All tool responses must be valid JSON strings that MCP clients can parse and display.
Error responses must use MCP's error format with clear human-readable messages.
The server must handle SIGINT/SIGTERM gracefully, cleaning up any active pipeline runs.
constraints: TypeScript strict mode, no any, no ts-ignore.
@modelcontextprotocol/sdk is the only new hard dependency.
MCP tools must reuse existing orchestrator functions -- no duplicating pipeline logic.
The MCP server must work when installed globally (npx agent-bober mcp) and locally (node dist/cli/index.js mcp).
